results.name: shell session start/log

results.name: daemon start/status/stop
results.evidence: Explicit `awod.exe` start reached `healthy`, repeated status stayed healthy, `repo list` worked through the daemon, `daemon stop` succeeded, and final status returned `not running`.

results.name: team init/plan/task/report/teardown/delete
results.evidence: The Windows checklist team flow completed end to end, including `pwd && ls` task execution reaching `review`, `team task supersede`, `team report`, `team teardown --force`, and `team delete`.

results.name: TUI startup/quit
results.evidence: `cmd.exe /c "echo q| C:\tmp\awo-validation\awo\target\debug\awo.exe"` exited 0 after the non-interactive quit-path fix.

notes: On Windows, normal CLI commands now stay in direct mode unless `awod` is already running explicitly. This avoids the redirected-output auto-start problems seen earlier while keeping explicit daemon workflows fully validated.
The older `windows_live_check.ps1` helper is still a stale debugging artifact and was not used as the final source of truth for this refreshed report.
